title: filago pygmaea subsp. linearifolia
intro: Filago pygmaea subsp. linearifolia, also known as dwarf cudweed, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, offering various health benefits such as antioxidant, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, which can aid in reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being. In herbal preparations, dwarf cudweed can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to treat minor injuries, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
